info.title: RegInforce AI Backend
info.description:
## RegInforce AI - Compliance Gap Analysis API
    
    This API provides document management and compliance gap analysis capabilities.
    
    ### Features
    
    * **Document Management**: Upload and process regulation and policy documents
    * **Gap Analysis**: AI-powered semantic analysis to identify compliance gaps
    * **Temporal Tracking**: Track gaps over time with status updates (NEW, EXISTING, RESOLVED, WORSENED)
    * **Anomaly Detection**: Detect unusual spikes in gaps or risk scores
    * **Policy Comparison**: Compare document versions and track changes
    * **Remediation Roadmap**: AI-powered remediation strategies with effort estimation
    * **Interactive Compliance Assistant**: ChatOps interface for compliance queries
    * **Caching**: Redis-based caching for fast report retrieval
    * **Rate Limiting**: Distributed rate limiting for API protection
    * **Async Processing**: Background task processing for long-running operations
    
    ### AI Providers
    
    Supports multiple AI providers through adapter pattern:
    - Google Gemini
    - OpenAI GPT
    - Anthropic Claude
    
    Configure via `AI_PROVIDER` environment variable.
    
    ### Workflow
    
    1. Upload regulation document (`POST /documents/upload`)
    2. Upload policy document (`POST /documents/upload`)
    3. Wait for processing to complete (check status via `GET /documents/{id}`)
    4. Run gap analysis (`POST /analysis/run`)
    5. Retrieve analysis report (`GET /analysis/{report_id}`)

post.description:
Upload a regulation or policy document for processing.

- Accepts PDF and TXT files
- Processing happens asynchronously in background
- Returns document metadata immediately

get.description:
Retrieve a document by its ID.

Returns document metadata including processing status.

List all uploaded documents.

- Returns up to `limit` documents
- Ordered by creation date (newest first)

schema.title: Response List Documents Api Documents  Get

post.description:
Trigger gap analysis between regulation and policy documents.

- Both documents must be in PROCESSED status
- Analysis runs asynchronously in background
- Returns report metadata immediately

get.description:
Retrieve an analysis report by ID.

- Checks Redis cache first for fast retrieval
- Falls back to database if not cached
- Returns report with all identified gaps

Retrieve historical gap analysis for the same document pair.

Shows how gaps have evolved over time across multiple analyses.

schema.title: Response Get History Api Analysis  Report Id  History Get

get.summary: Get compliance trend analysis with anomaly detection
get.description:
Get trend analysis showing improvement or degradation over time.

Optionally includes anomaly detection to identify unusual patterns.

Useful for:
- Visualizing compliance progress
- Identifying trends (improving, stable, degrading)
- Detecting unusual spikes or changes
- Alerting on critical deviations

Detect statistical anomalies in compliance gap analysis.

Analyzes historical data to identify unusual spikes in:
- Total gap count
- Average risk scores
- High-severity gap count

Uses z-score and IQR-based methods to detect deviations from normal patterns.

post.description:
Compare current analysis with the previous analysis.

Shows:
- Summary statistics (gap counts, risk scores)
- Resolved gaps (present in previous, absent in current)
- New gaps (absent in previous, present in current)
- Worsened gaps (risk increased)
- Existing gaps (unchanged)

get.description:
Compare two policy document versions and show differences.

Supports multiple output formats:
- **json**: Structured diff data (default)
- **html**: HTML formatted diff for web display
- **markdown**: Markdown formatted diff for documentation
- **unified**: Git-style unified diff format

The comparison shows:
- Additions (new text in newer version)
- Deletions (text removed from older version)
- Modifications (text changed between versions)
- Overall similarity percentage

parameters.name: format

schema.description: Output format: json, html, markdown, unified
schema.title: Format

parameters.description: Output format: json, html, markdown, unified

post.description:
Generate a remediation plan for an analysis report.

Creates AI-powered remediation strategies for all gaps in the report,
with effort estimation, prioritization, and dependency analysis.

Args:
    report_id: Analysis report ID
    request: Optional generation parameters (organization size, industry, target days)
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Generated remediation plan with all steps

get.description:
Get the remediation plan for an analysis report.

Args:
    report_id: Analysis report ID
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Remediation plan if exists

Get a remediation plan by ID.

Args:
    plan_id: Remediation plan ID
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Remediation plan

Get dependency graph for a remediation plan.

Shows relationships between steps, critical path, and parallelizable groups.

Args:
    plan_id: Remediation plan ID
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Dependency graph with nodes and relationships

Export remediation plan in various formats.

Supported formats:
- json: Complete structured data
- csv: Spreadsheet-compatible format
- jira: Jira-compatible JSON for import
- markdown: Human-readable documentation

Args:
    plan_id: Remediation plan ID
    format: Export format (json, csv, jira, markdown)
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Exported plan in requested format

patch.description:
Update the status of a remediation step.

Automatically updates timestamps (started_at, completed_at) based on status transitions.

Args:
    step_id: Remediation step ID
    update: Status update
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Updated remediation step

get.description:
List all policy templates, optionally filtered by category.

Args:
    category: Optional category filter (encryption, retention, access_control, etc.)
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    List of policy templates ordered by usage count

schema.title: Response List Policy Templates Api Remediation Templates Get

post.description:
Create a new policy template.

Args:
    template_data: Template data
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Created policy template

get.description:
Get a policy template by ID.

Args:
    template_id: Template ID
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    Policy template

delete.description:
Delete a policy template.

Args:
    template_id: Template ID
    session: Database session
    
Returns:
    No content on success

post.description:
Send a message in a conversation.

- If conversation_id is None, creates a new conversation
- Validates message scope and safety
- Classifies intent and extracts entities
- Generates AI response
- Returns complete conversation state

**Security:**
- Blocks off-topic messages (non-compliance queries)
- Detects jailbreak attempts and prompt injection
- Auto-suspends conversations after 5 blocked messages in 5 minutes

get.description:
Get detailed conversation information.

- Returns conversation metadata
- Optionally includes full message history
- Includes current context state

delete.description:
Delete a conversation (soft delete - archives it).

- Soft delete: sets is_archived = True
- Preserves data for audit trail
- Messages remain in database

get.description:
List conversations with filtering and pagination.

- Filter by user_id (multi-tenancy)
- Optionally include archived/suspended conversations
- Paginated results (default 50 per page, max 200)
- Ordered by last_activity (newest first)

post.description:
Suspend a conversation due to misuse.

- Prevents further messages
- Used for abuse prevention
- Can be reversed by unsuspending

Archive a conversation.

- Marks conversation as archived
- Removes from active list
- Can be retrieved later if needed

get.description:
Get messages for a conversation.

- Returns up to `limit` messages (default 100, max 500)
- Ordered by timestamp (oldest first)
- Includes both user and assistant messages

schema.title: Response Get Conversation Messages Api Chat Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Get

get.description:
Health check endpoint.

Returns basic API information.

Detailed health check endpoint.

Returns health status of all components.
